axiom_catalog.label: Free Will Axiom
axiom_catalog.statement: Human beings possess free will. Every rational agent, even in the act of disagreeing, is forced to presuppose it.
axiom_catalog.note: Described as the single starting principle; self-referentially necessary; the foundation of all subsequent derivations.

axiom_catalog.label: Property-as-Liberty Axiom
axiom_catalog.statement: Liberty, in its objective form, is individual property rights. Property begins with the human body.
axiom_catalog.note: Presented as the first derived definition from A-000001; treated as axiomatic throughout the text.

axiom_catalog.label: Divine Origin of Rights Axiom
axiom_catalog.statement: Individual property rights require a divine source to withstand the state, the majority, and power. Without a transcendent grounding, rights cannot resist violation.
axiom_catalog.note: Used to bridge from libertarianism (incomplete) to religion (necessary).

axiom_catalog.label: Tawhid Axiom
axiom_catalog.statement: God is one; no servitude to anything other than God is legitimate. Every form of servitude to other-than-God is a violation of liberty.
axiom_catalog.note: The meta-axiom of the religious formal system. All other religious principles derive from or are consistent with this.

axiom_catalog.label: Non-Compulsion Axiom
axiom_catalog.statement: There is no compulsion in religion ('La Ikraha fi al-Din'). Any religion that compels is distorted.
axiom_catalog.note: Quranic axiom used to distinguish authentic from false religion.

axiom_catalog.label: Resurrection Axiom
axiom_catalog.statement: Divine accountability (resurrection) exists after death. This is the mechanism that makes liberty stable: without it, the 'last round problem' in game-theoretic deterrence collapses.
axiom_catalog.note: Depends on A-000004. Required to solve the game-theory problem of deterrence.

axiom_catalog.label: Prophethood Axiom
axiom_catalog.statement: Prophethood is a real divine institution and it has been finalized (sealed). No new prophet will come.
axiom_catalog.note: Required to prevent fraud and proliferation of false religions. Depends on A-000004.

axiom_catalog.label: Rule of Taslīṭ Axiom
axiom_catalog.statement: People have dominion (taslīṭ) over their own property. This is the religious codification of individual property rights.
axiom_catalog.note: Functions as the operational axiom for evaluating all jurisprudential narrations.

axiom_catalog.label: Dignitary Equality Axiom
axiom_catalog.statement: All human beings possess equal dignity. This is a faith-based truth prior to any scientific conclusion.
axiom_catalog.note: Used to argue against state-engineered discrimination and affirmative action alike.

axiom_catalog.label: Personal Accountability Axiom
axiom_catalog.statement: Penalty must be personally accountable: only the violator bears the punishment, not his family, community, or successors.
axiom_catalog.note: Component of divine justice; used to critique collective punishment by the state.

axiom_catalog.label: Finitude of Axioms Axiom
axiom_catalog.statement: A valid formal system must have a finite and minimal number of axioms. Proliferating axioms leads to contradiction, mysticism, and totalitarianism.
axiom_catalog.note: Methodological axiom governing the architecture of the whole theory.

axiom_catalog.label: Consistency Axiom
axiom_catalog.statement: A valid system must be internally consistent (non-contradictory). Any interpretation that produces contradictions with liberty is to be rejected.
axiom_catalog.note: Methodological axiom; derived from Gödel's framework as applied to formal systems.

axiom_catalog.label: Priority of Liberty Axiom
axiom_catalog.statement: Liberty and property rights are prior to any scientific conclusion. They are normative axioms, not scientific findings.
axiom_catalog.note: Used against positivism; establishes the normative primacy of liberty over empirical claims.

axiom_catalog.label: Theory-Over-Data Axiom
axiom_catalog.statement: Theory has primacy over data in understanding history and religion. Without theory, raw data cannot be interpreted.
axiom_catalog.note: Applied to Shahnameh interpretation, historical analysis, and religious jurisprudence.

axiom_catalog.label: Mahdism Necessity Axiom
axiom_catalog.statement: Without the doctrine of the Hidden Imam (Mahdism/Advent), a consistent theory of liberty cannot be completed. The society awaiting the Advent gradually recedes the state.
axiom_catalog.note: Used to complete the logical circle of the theory within Shi'i framework.
